mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) across all periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and boosts profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand expansion, which increases corporate revenues and yields positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.05) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off sentiment and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during supply-constrained environments.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of weeks following negative INDPRO growth, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 2.5 percent force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which raises input costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in > 55% of observed months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained economic expansion that directly lifts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward quarterly returns are negative in > 60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.04 across all rolling test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and federal funds rate hikes indicates policy-induced supply drag, which triggers immediate sell-offs and negative weekly S&P 500 returns during tightening phases.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are positive in > 50% of weeks where the divergence condition holds, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.
